headline: Model Context Protocol breaks out as the connective standard for agent deployment across enterprise and web tooling.
body: Model Context Protocol is the dominant breakout this week, anchoring tutorials and architectures from client-server JSON-RPC fundamentals to enterprise agent configuration over Looker, Snowflake, and Notion. A parallel WebMCP proposal would let websites expose typed, named tools directly to agents instead of forcing DOM parsing, now in early preview on Chrome 146. Agent orchestration scales alongside it, with running hundreds of coding agents at once and offline dispatch to isolated git worktrees gated by verification. System architecture, transformer architecture, RLHF, cross-modal alignment, and KV cache quantization all move together as improving techniques, while text-to-CAD and faithful generation appear as new entrants.
